title: Nezikchened
wikitext:
{{External|rs|rsc}} {{Otheruses|the quest boss fought during [[Legends' Quest]]|the [[Nightmare Zone]] variant|Nezikchened (Nightmare Zone)}} {{Switch infobox |text1 = Normal |item1 = {{Infobox Monster |bucketname = Normal |name = Nezikchened |image = [[File:Nezikchened.png|250px]] |release = [[20 August]] [[2003]] |update = New - Legends Quest! |members = Yes |combat = 187 |size = 3 |examine = An ancient, powerful demon of the underworld. |xpbonus = 0 |attributes = demon |max hit = 18 (Melee & Magic), ~20 (Dark Dagger) |aggressive = Yes |poisonous = No |attack style = [[Slash]], [[Magic]] |attack speed = 5 |hitpoints = 150 |att = 165 |str = 168 |def = 167 |mage = 160 |range = 160 |attbns = 0 |strbns = 0 |amagic = 0 |mbns = 0 |arange = 0 |rngbns = 0 |dstab = 0 |dslash = 0 |dcrush = 0 |dmagic = 0 |drange = 0 |elementalweaknesstype = Water |elementalweaknesspercent = 10 |immunepoison = No |immunevenom = No |id = 3962 |leagueRegion = Karamja }} |text2 = Echned Zekin |item2 = {{Infobox NPC |name = Echned Zekin |image = [[File:Echned Zekin.png|275px]] |release = [[20 August]] [[2003]] |update = New - Legends Quest! |members = Yes |race = [[Ghost]] |quest = [[Legends' Quest]] |location = [[Viyeldi Caves]] |gender = Male |options = Talk-to |examine = A spirit of the underworld. |map = no |id = 3968 |leagueRegion = Karamja }} [[File:Echned Zekin chathead.png|left]] }} '''Nezikchened''' is a level 187 [[black demon]] that has to be defeated three different times during the [[Legends' Quest]]. He is also among the strongest demons in the game. At the start of each fight he drains the player's prayer points. Like all demons, he is also vulnerable to the effects of [[demonbane weapons]]. He has a very peculiar habit of calling the player 'Vacu.' According to [[Gujuo]], 'Vacu' was the name for shamans who could summon powerful and wise spirits, but were enslaved by the spirits for asking too much of them. Despite being a black demon, he is not considered one for the purpose of a [[Slayer task]]. He can be fought in the [[Nightmare Zone]] after the quest is complete. ==First battle== Nezikchened has possessed [[Ungadulu]], the shaman of the [[Kharazi tribe]]. Players must use the [[binding book]] on him to reveal his true form. By splashing Ungadulu with [[holy water]] before using the binding book, Nezikchened will start the fight weakened. He will then drain the player's prayer and start attacking with melee, or magic if the player is out of reach. He can be easily defeated if [[Protect from Melee]] is used while standing next to him. Attacking him with [[demonbane weapons]] or throwing a vial of [[holy water]] at him can weaken him. This is the easiest of Nezikchened's three phases, as it is very predictable. The recommended attack style for players is ranged due to its inherent accuracy, but melee works nearly as well. It's not recommended to use magic unless you have a high magic level (75+), as his magic level of 160 makes him resistant to non-demonbane spells. After defeating him, he will hit the player with a magic attack resembling [[fire bolt]] before disappearing, which can do upwards of 20 damage. ==Second battle== Nezikchened appears again, this time disguised as the ghost Echned Zekin, an anagram for Nezikchened, and will ask players to kill the spirit [[Viyeldi]]. Players who do so will save time initially, but will have to fight [[Ranalph Devere]], [[Irvig Senay]] and [[San Tojalon]] in the final battle. Players who do not wish to kill Viyeldi can go back and speak to [[Ungadulu]], who will give the player a [[Holy force]] card, which can be used to weaken Nezikchened. Whichever path they choose, when players return to Echned Zekin, he will turn into Nezikchened and fight the player. Just like before, he drains the player's prayer, unless they use the holy force spell card on him first. This fight is relatively similar to the first one in terms of difficulty. ===Dark dagger=== One time during the fight, he will throw a [[dark dagger]] at the player which can deal a decent amount of damage (up to 20). The player will be notified of this attack via the demon saying {{overhead|Ha, ha, ha... Feel my power!}} and a chat message {{mes|The demon takes out a dark dagger and throws it at you...}}. The attack will either hit or be dodged, depending on the player's [[Agility]] level. If the attack is dodged, it is accompanied with the chat message {{mes|But you neatly manage to dodge the attack.}}, or if it hits, {{mes|The dagger hits you with an agonising blow.}}. {{Skilling success chart|label=Dodging the dagger throw|xlabel=Agility level|showbefore=no |label1=Success chance|low1=0|high1=254|req1=50|image1=Dark dagger.png }} ==Final battle== At the end of the quest, players must fight Nezikchened one last time. Before beginning the fight, it's recommended to turn on [[Protect from Melee]], as the demon will get a few free hits while the player is stalled. He will, like before, drain prayer at the beginning and then proceed to attack with melee or a hard hitting magical attack if the player is out of melee range. An effective way to defeat the demon involves safespotting him by hiding in the trees nearby and attacking him with ranged. Melee can also be used safely for this phase with Protect from Melee active. If players chose the "short" route during the quest and killed [[Viyeldi]], he will also spawn [[Ranalph Devere]], [[Irvig Senay]], and [[San Tojalon]] one at a time before facing the player himself. Make sure your prayer points are high and Protect from Melee is on after killing each warrior, as Nezikchened will again get a few free hits before summoning the next warrior or facing you himself. After the player has delivered the final blow to Nezikchened, he will exclaim {{overhead|To be beaten by a mere mortal... I will have... my... revenge...}} and the player sarcastically responds {{overhead|Yeah, yeah, whatever.}} and {{overhead|Heard it all before.}} right before Nezikchened is defeated for good. ==Dialogue== {{hastranscript|speaksin}} ==Changes== {{Subject changes header}} {{Subject changes |date = 22 January 2007 |update = The Lair of Tarn Razorlor |change = Echned Zekin was graphically updated. }} {{Subject changes |date = 29 August 2006 |update = Demons, Dragons and Giants |change = Nezikchened was graphically updated. }} {{Subject changes footer}} ===Gallery (historical)=== Nezikchened= {|style="text-align: center" cellpadding="15" |[[File:Nezikchened (historical).png|center|300x150px]] |[[File:Nezikchened.png|center|300x150px]] |- |29 March 2004 –
29 August 2006 |29 August 2006 –
present |} |-| Echned Zekin= {|style="text-align: center" cellpadding="15" |[[File:Echned Zekin (historical).png|center|300x150px]] |[[File:Echned Zekin.png|center|300x150px]] |- |[[File:Echned Zekin chathead (historical).png|center]] |[[File:Echned Zekin chathead.png|center]] |- |29 March 2004 –
22 January 2007 |22 January 2007 –
present |} == Trivia == * [[Nezikchened's mum]] was an unobtainable item that existed in the game's cache from 2005 until 2014, when it was renamed to Null. The examine text states that "She wasn't pleased to see what her son did." * Nezikchened's magic spell resembles that of a [[earth strike]] via sound and, in-part, its texture, but the texture's colour is black and the damage is higher than the "strike" tier spells. The impact animation is also different, which looks something similar to the [[Flames of Zamorak]] spell, but a little larger in scale and with flames having a blue and purple tinge - in addition to orange and white embers floating around and above the player. * Unlike other demons, Nezikchened does not drop any type of [[demonic ashes]] upon being defeated. He also does not drop regular [[ashes]], like demons used to do prior to the release of demonic ashes. However, the [[Nightmare Zone]] version of Nezikchened still drops ashes. {{Legends' Quest}} {{Demons}} [[Category:Bosses]] [[Category:Quest monsters]]
